jcr:content.jcr:description: The AbbVie Foundation is a [501(c)(3)] nonprofit committed to driving transformative change in communities worldwide and advancing health equity so that every individual can realize their full potential.
jcr:content.jcr:title: AbbVie Foundation
jcr:content.ctaAltText: Learn more about philanthropy at AbbVie
jcr:content.description: We collaborate with our partners to address the many factors that influence the health and well-being of people in their communities.
jcr:content.title: AbbVie Foundation

text.text: The AbbVie Foundation is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit working to drive transformative change in communities worldwide so that everyone can live their healthiest life.

text_copy.text: We are committed to advancing health equity in communities through innovative, multi-year partnerships and through our employee impact programs around the world.

text_copy_copy.text:
The AbbVie Foundation believes that transformative change begins in communities. We listen and learn from our partners, empowering them to develop innovative solutions that will address critical disparities and advance health equity in their communities. Since 2013, the AbbVie Foundation has provided $389 million in grants to more than 310 organizations.

We collaborate closely with our partners to address the unique needs of their communities, with a focus on removing systemic barriers, increasing access to care and investing in innovative solutions that have the greatest potential to drive change.

text_copy.text: In collaboration with organizations like CARE, we advance new approaches to address the many factors that influence a person’s health and well-being, including access, education, social, financial and environmental conditions – disrupting systemic barriers and expanding health equity in underserved communities.

button_copy.jcr:title: Learn more
button_copy.accessibilityLabel: Learn more about our inovative impact

text_copy_copy.text: We partner with nonprofits to strengthen community-centered health systems and increase equitable access to quality health care. Together with CommunityHealth, we opened a microsite clinic, bringing essential health services to underserved residents on Chicago’s West Side. And we’re supporting the University of Chicago Medicine's new cancer pavilion, which will bring high-quality cancer care to Chicago's South Side.

text_copy_copy_copy.text: Through our partnerships with MATTER and Health Leads, we help accelerate innovative, community-led programs and pilot new solutions addressing the most pressing and complex health equity challenges of today.

button.jcr:title: Learn more
button.accessibilityLabel: Learn more about our inovative impact

text_copy_copy_copy_.text:
In addition to our multi-year partnerships, the AbbVie Foundation is committed to supporting our communities through our local grants programs, which are designed to respond to complex community needs. We value the deep understanding that our local grants partners have of their communities, to ensure meaningful, lasting impact.

The grants programs include:

  • Employee Resource Group (ERG) Community Grants – providing $700,000 in funding annually to nonprofit organizations that align with the Foundation's mission to advance health equity. Organizations must be based and operate in the United States as a 501(c)(3), with preference given to organizations working near  AbbVie U.S. locations.
  • Local Community Grants – providing nearly $1,500,000 in funding annually to support eligible nonprofit organizations in 8 locations, including: Barceloneta, Puerto Rico; Cambridge, Massachusetts; Irvine, California; North Chicago, Illinois; San Juan, Puerto Rico; South San Francisco, California; Waco, Texas; and Worcester, Massachusetts.
We are not currently accepting unsolicited grant applications. If you would like to learn more about future opportunities, please click the button below to provide your contact information.

text_copy_copy.text:
AbbVie employees are passionate about making a difference in their communities. Around the globe, our employees give generously of their time, expertise and resources to deliver on our shared purpose and deepen the AbbVie Foundation’s collective impact.

Our employees directly support the AbbVie Foundation’s mission to advance health equity through our industry-leading giving, volunteering and employee support programs.

text_copy.text: AbbVie employee resource groups (ERGs) play an important role in our giving, awarding $700,000 annually in Foundation-funded grants to nonprofit organizations advancing health equity. Through the Employee Giving Campaign, we match employee donations to eligible nonprofits they are passionate about. Since the program’s inception, employees have raised $185 million+ with matching from the AbbVie Foundation.

text_copy_copy.text: Our employee volunteering programs, Week of Possibilities and Team Possibilities, unite colleagues around the world by helping advance health equity through community service. Since our founding, AbbVie employees have volunteered more than 428,000 hours in communities around the world.

text_copy_copy_copy.text: We are committed to assisting our people and their families in times of need. The AbbVie Employee Relief Program provides critical financial relief to employees after disasters and personal hardship and the AbbVie Possibilities Scholarship Program alleviates the financial strain of tuition costs for children of AbbVie employees.

text_copy_copy.text: Week of Possibilities, the AbbVie Foundation’s annual global volunteering event, is an inspiring time when thousands of employees come together to serve our local communities. Volunteer activities align with the AbbVie Foundation’s mission to advance health equity and create a remarkable, long-lasting impact around the world. In 2025, more than 14,000 employees across nearly 58 countries and territories volunteered nearly 44,000 hours.
